mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of recovery-phase observations or predictive power vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of high-inflation months or correlation coefficient exceeds negative 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent annually signals severe demand weakness and recessionary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of sub-0.5% growth quarters or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the federal funds rate indicate accommodative policy supporting job growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60% of instances or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals abrupt supply chain disruptions or demand shocks, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered months or signal fails to beat baseline in out-of-sample testing.
